paragraph.content: No data available for this section

content.content:
Interpretive Data: NOT DETECTED result does not preclude COVID-19 and should not be used as the sole basis for patient management decisions. Non detected result must be combined with clinical observations, patient history, and epidemiological information.
DETECTED result indicates that RNA from SARS-CoV-2 was detected and suggests the diagnosis of COVID-19. Clinical correlation with patient history and other diagnostic information is necessary to determine patient infection status. If requested by public health authorities, specimens will be sent for additional testing.
Invalid:
Presence or absence of SARS CoV-2 cannot be determined. Repeat assay with new sample.
Transmission based precautions for a detected result:
***Special Droplet + Contact Precautions required. For discontinuation, refer to current Virginia Mason Standard Work.***
Transmission based precautions for a not detected or invalid result:
***Standard Precautions required, including mask and eye protection when patient is symptomatic with respiratory infection presentation. Per organizational expectations during COVID-19 response – medical mask and eye protection are required for all patient interactions. ***
Assay Information:
This assay is performed using the Abbott Alinity m SARS-CoV-2 assay, a real-time (rt) reverse transcriptase (RT) polymerase chain reaction (PCR) test intended for the qualitative detection of nucleic acid from the SARS-CoV-2 in nasopharyngeal (NP) and nasal mid-turbinate swab specimens from patients suspected of COVID-19. The Abbott Alinity m SARS-CoV-2 assay is a dual target assay for the RdRp and N genes. This assay has been validated by Virginia Mason Clinical Microbiology Laboratory, a CLIA high-complexity clinical laboratory, for in vitro diagnostic use under FDA Emergency Use Authorization only.

Virginia Mason Medical Center Laboratory

1100 9th Avenue
Seattle, WA 98101-2756 US

item.paragraph:
Partridge MD, Rebecca: PERFORM, MODIFY
Event Display: PEDS Outpt Clinic Note
Authored Date: 20220924151726-0700

Assessment/Plan
_
Fever R50.9
Ordered:
SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19) Qual PCR (VMMC) AMB
4 year old healthy immunized child with URI symptoms with fever.
Looks amazing on exam, very active and playful, climbing
No abnormalities on exam
Discussed watchful waiting vs. getting CXR now.
Decided to wait as his exam is completely normal
If still having fever in 2-3 days, please call.
Should be reassessed with consideration of CXR and blood
Call for any increasing difficulty breathing.
COVID negative.
Chief Complaint
Cough/sore throat/fever
History of Present Illness
Healthy 4 year old presents with fever, cough, sore throat
Illness started at least 6 days ago, Tmax 102, taking acetaminophen
persistent cough. Stuffy nose for a couple of days
Some complaints about tummy pain, mom wonders if he isn't getting enough fiber
Had a temp to 102 this morning, has gone over 100.5 every day.
Eating pretty well, drinking well
Sleeping pretty well
Had 2 days of a mild rash on his wrist
No other rashes
No red eyes, red lips.
Last COVID test 2 weeks ago
Physical Exam
Vitals & Measurements
T: 98.1 °F
WT: 15.6 kg
Head: Atraumatic, normocephalic
Eyes: PERRL, EOMs intact, normal red reflex
ENT: Tympanic membranes are clear, Oral mucosa is moist, No pharyngeal erythema, Normal mouth and pharynx.
Neck: Supple, Non-tender, No lymphadenopathy.
Respiratory: Lungs are clear to auscultation, Respirations are non-labored, Breath sounds are equal, Symmetrical chest wall expansion.
Cardiovascular: Normal rate, Regular rhythm, No murmur, Good pulses equal in all extremities.
Gastrointestinal: Soft, Non-tender, No organomegaly.
Musculoskeletal: Normal range of motion, Normal strength, No scoliosis, full ROM hips, knees.
Integumentary: Warm, Dry, Pink, No rash.
Neurologic: Alert, Oriented, No focal deficits, Normal deep tendon reflexes.
Psychiatric: Cooperative, Appropriate mood & affect.
External Vitals & Measurements
No qualifying data available.
Growth Percentiles
No qualifying data available.
Hearing and Vision Screenings
No qualifying data available.
Problem List/Past Medical History
Ongoing
Abdominal pain
Birth mark Comments: forehead
Chromosomal microduplication
Hemangioma
Prematurity
Strabismus
Historical
No qualifying data
Medications
Albuterol (Eqv-ProAir HFA) 90 mcg/inh inhalation aerosol, 1 puff(s), ORAL INH, Q6 Hours, PRN
Vitamin D, Daily
Allergies
NKA
Social History
Home/Environment
Lives with Father, Mother, Siblings, Grandmother.
Family History
Allergies: Mother and Father.
Anxiety: Mother.
Depression: Mother.
Diabetes mellitus type 2: Sister.
Eczema: Father and Pat. GFather.
Health Maintenance
Pending (in the next year)
OverDue
Vaccine - influenza ("flu shot") due 08/24/22 and every 200 day(s)
Due In Future
Ped - well child check not due until 11/11/22 and every 1 year(s)
Satisfied (in the past 1 year)
Satisfied
Ped - well child check on 11/11/21.
Vaccine - influenza ("flu shot") on 10/01/21. Satisfied by Nguyen RN, Jade H
(Electronically Signed on 09/28/22 12:08 PM)
Partridge MD, Rebecca
